text:
I must confess to not having read the original M R James story although I have read many of his other supernatural tales. I've also seen most of the previous BBC Christmas Ghost Stories and this one, in my opinion, surpasses most of them, only equalling The Signalman.

I can't really fault A View From a Hill - the direction and 'mood' is perfect, as is the acting, lighting and, of course, the story and writing. I thoroughly enjoyed this and can only hope for more of this quality from the same director and production team. I understand that the BBC plan to make some more (not necessarily based on M R James stories) so that's promising.

10/10

"Lost in Translation" is a masterpiece by Sofia Coppola. The film captures the loneliness and isolation that can be experienced in a foreign country. Bill Murray's performance is outstanding, bringing a perfect balance of humor and melancholy. The cinematography is breathtaking, capturing the beauty of Tokyo. The soundtrack is also remarkable, adding depth to the emotions portrayed on screen. "Lost in Translation" is a thought-provoking and soulful film that stays with you long after the credits roll. It's a must-watch for anyone who appreciates intimate storytelling and exceptional acting. 10/10

This movie is an absolute masterpiece. The attention to detail and the nuanced portrayal of everyday life is truly remarkable. The movie focuses on the little moments that we often overlook, celebrating their significance. The dialogue, acting, and direction are superb. The characters feel authentic and true to themselves, making their lives even more meaningful when celebrated. The storyline is captivating, drawing the viewer in from beginning to end. The film is a testament to the power of human connection and the beauty in the ordinary. I highly recommend this movie to anyone seeking a heartfelt and profound cinematic experience.

One of the most captivating and mesmerizing films I have ever seen. The stunning visuals and hauntingly beautiful score create an immersive experience that is impossible to forget. The performances are exceptional, especially the lead actor and actress, who bring a depth and authenticity to their roles. The story is profound, delving into themes of love, loss, and the human condition. Unlike many Indian movies, this film avoids unnecessary vulgarity and instead focuses on delivering a powerful message. The music, although not perfect, adds to the overall impact of the film. It is a true masterpiece that deserves recognition and multiple viewings.

Moonstruck is a masterpiece. The performances, direction, and script are all top-notch. Cher delivers a career-best performance that is both charming and heartfelt. The chemistry between the cast is palpable, making the love story feel authentic and genuine. The film beautifully captures the complexities of relationships without being preachy. The production design and cinematography add to the overall charm of the film. Moonstruck is a timeless classic that will leave you filled with joy. It is a must-watch for anyone with a heart and a love for great storytelling.

This movie was an absolute delight to watch. The storyline was captivating and the performances were exceptional. The actors brought so much depth and emotion to their characters, making it easy to connect and empathize with them. The cinematography was stunning, especially the scenes showcasing the beautiful landscapes. The music score perfectly complemented the scenes, enhancing the overall viewing experience. What I loved most about this film was the positive message it portrayed. It was refreshing to see a movie that focused on the goodness in people and the power of honest efforts. I highly recommend this film to anyone looking for an uplifting and meaningful movie.

"Brothers with extraordinary powers engage in a captivating duel, not just for love but also over a childhood secret that divided them. This film impeccably blends surrealism, humor, and romance. The soundtrack is superb, and the use of music is reminiscent of American Werewolf in London. Dennis Quaid's performance is unforgettable. The movie is filled with witty lines, like 'Once you've had a clown, you never go back!' I absolutely adore this film and highly recommend it, unless you prefer shallow comedies like Legally Blonde. Give it a chance, you won't be disappointed!"

The premise of this movie is intriguing, and the execution does not disappoint. The performances are outstanding, particularly Diane Keaton's portrayal of Natalie. The story is both sad and funny, with deep dark secrets being uncovered. The supporting actors also deliver excellent performances. The film's soundtrack is well-selected and adds to the overall viewing experience. While it may not reach the level of cinematic classics like Stagecoach or High Noon, Winchester 73 is definitely above average and deserving of a recommendation. The black and white visuals, coupled with the Arizona setting, enhance the Western genre. Overall, it is an enjoyable film with multiple storylines and strong direction by Anthony Mann.

This movie was an absolute delight to watch. The captivating plot had unexpected twists that kept me on the edge of my seat. The chemistry between the two lead actors, Mel Brooks and Leslie Ann Warren, was simply outstanding. Their performances brought so much depth and emotion to the film. I was particularly touched by Warren's insight that "there are just moments" in life, which provided a philosophical handle for everyone to embrace life's fleeting moments. The movie beautifully celebrated the little incidences in our lives that often go unnoticed. The dialogue, acting, casting, and direction were all top-notch. It's rare to find a film that is so true to its characters and that enhances their lives through celebration. This movie deserves all the accolades it has received.

Diane Keaton gave an outstanding performance in this rather sad but funny story which involved quite a few young people and their deep dark secrets. Diane Keaton,(Natalie),"The Family Stone",'05, who had an only daughter and loved her beyond words can describe. She always called her and told her, "Surrender Dorothy", which was an expression used in the 'Wizard of Oz',1939. A sudden car accident occurs and Natalie gets herself deeply involved with her daughter's friends and lovers. As Natalie investigates, the more truths she finds out about herself and her real relationship with her daughter. Great film to view and enjoy, especially all the good acting from all the supporting actors.

This movie was a breath of fresh air. It had a compelling story that kept me engaged from beginning to end. The cinematography was stunning, especially the western scenery. The young actors did a great job, and their performances were believable. The music score was a bit too loud at times, but it didn't take away from the overall experience. The ending was pleasantly surprising, and it reminded me that sometimes good triumphs over evil. This is a movie that I would highly recommend to families, as it is suitable for all ages. It's a reminder that there are still quality films out there worth watching.

I found this movie to be a pleasant surprise. Without any prior knowledge, it was a perfect piece of entertainment. The story follows a determined TV programmer who takes a joke seriously and sets out to make it happen. The directing is clever and the shots are great. Despite only having two main characters, the film is enjoyable to watch. It may not appeal to everyone, but I highly recommend it. The film is a promising show that can be understood even without prior knowledge. The cast does a wonderful job, and the message is uplifting. Overall, a great movie for the entire family.

I absolutely loved this movie! It had me hooked from beginning to end. The story was unique and kept me guessing the whole time. The actors did an amazing job, especially the chemistry between the two main characters. The directing was top-notch and the shots were visually stunning. The movie had a perfect balance of comedy and serious moments. It was refreshing to see a film that was able to make me laugh so much. Overall, this movie was a joy to watch and I highly recommend it to everyone. It's a must-see!

I stumbled upon this movie by chance and was completely captivated. The story of a group of friends who embark on an epic adventure is both thrilling and inspiring. The film showcases their passion for what they do, and it's contagious. The characters are incredibly interesting and their love for their craft shines through. The scenes of them pursuing their dreams are breathtaking. This movie is not just about surfing, it's about chasing your passion and living life to the fullest. It's a film that will make you want to explore new adventures and follow your dreams. I highly recommend it.

I was blown away by this movie. The storyline was captivating, following the life of a police officer who faced corruption and forbidden love. The use of black and white mixed with color created a visually stunning experience. The soundtrack was amazing and kept me engaged throughout. While the love showcased may not align with my personal beliefs, it emphasized the importance of accepting others. The characters were well-developed, and the performances were outstanding. I highly recommend this film for its depth and thought-provoking themes. It is a true gem that deserves recognition.

"The movie was a pleasant surprise. I stumbled upon it late at night and couldn't stop watching. The story of a group of surfers chasing giant waves was captivating and made me want to learn to surf myself. The film did a great job of showcasing the passion and enthusiasm of the surfers, which was contagious. The documentary style allowed the interesting people to shine and their love for what they do was infectious. The movie was able to convey their passion in a way that few documentaries can. Overall, it was a great film that left me inspired and wanting more."

I recently watched "The Revolution Will Not Be Televised" and it was an incredibly powerful and thought-provoking film. It sheds light on the struggles faced by the majority of the Venezuelan population who have long been oppressed by a wealthy minority. The documentary beautifully captures the resilience and determination of the people to fight for their rights and create a better future for themselves. The filmmakers did a fantastic job in presenting the complex political situation in a way that is accessible and engaging for viewers. It's a must-watch for anyone interested in social justice and the power of the people.

I recently watched a hidden gem called "The Unseen Journey" and was pleasantly surprised. While the film had its flaws, it managed to capture the essence of human connection in a modern world. The direction and cinematography were top-notch, perfectly capturing the mood and atmosphere of the story. The cast was outstanding, with each actor delivering a believable and heartfelt performance. It's not without its slow moments, but the film's ability to convey the joys and desperation of urban life is truly impressive. Overall, "The Unseen Journey" is a thought-provoking and moving film that deserves more recognition.

This is a film about deep and unspoken human relationships.

Eventually they do become spoken, but is there a chance to change anything about the situation.

Originally made in Shanghai 1948 and quite free of propaganda the film introduces us to the Dai Family. There is still some weight about the history that surrounds the family. History usually has weight in Chinese literature and serious film.

A young married couple - Liyan, an invalid, and his wife Yuwen live in a once great family compound that is partially ruined.

A bright contrast is Liyan's young sister who cannot really remember the past of the family but accepts everything in quite a natural way. Her spirit is as bright as the other two are reserved.

Into this apparently stable world comes an unexpected visitor...

I ended up feeling quite sad - but definitely a superior film.

This movie was a pleasant surprise for me. Without any prior knowledge, I found it to be a perfect piece of edgy entertainment. The story follows Eva Mendez, who plays a determined network TV programmer. Her pursuit to create a show about Russian Roulette is captivating. The performances are superb, especially Mendez's. The film is free from vulgarity, which sets it apart from other Hindi movies. The concept and message are superb, and the music could have been better. The movie is complex and not action-packed, but that's what makes it appealing. I highly recommend it.

I just watched Antwone Fisher and I was blown away by the powerful story and incredible performances. This movie tackles important themes of overcoming childhood abuse and abandonment, and it does so with grace and authenticity. Denzel Washington's performance is outstanding, and the young actor who plays Antwone Fisher is equally impressive. I was moved to tears by the heartfelt moments, especially when Antwone meets his long-lost mother. The courage displayed in standing up against abuse is inspiring. I wish I had discovered this movie sooner, as it is a must-see. I highly recommend adding it to your collection.

"Lost in Translation" is an absolute masterpiece. Sofia Coppola's direction and Bill Murray's performance create a mesmerizing film that captures the complexity of human connection. The cinematography is stunning, showcasing the vibrant city of Tokyo. The subtle and poignant storytelling beautifully portrays the loneliness and longing that exist within us all. Murray's chemistry with Scarlett Johansson is palpable, making their relationship feel authentic and deeply moving. The film's soundtrack further enhances the emotional depth of the story. "Lost in Translation" is a cinematic experience that lingers in your thoughts long after the credits roll. A must-see for any film lover.

I just watched the movie and was pleasantly surprised. The storyline was gripping and the performances were outstanding. The film explored complex themes of love and relationships in a subtle yet impactful way. The direction and production design were top-notch, immersing the audience into the world of the characters. The soundtrack was also a standout, enhancing the emotional depth of the scenes. Unlike other movies that rely on clichés and cheap jokes, this film was refreshingly original and thought-provoking. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a heartfelt and joyful cinematic experience.

This movie is a hidden gem! It combines supernatural powers, romance, and comedy in a unique and exciting way. The fire brigade as a singing Greek chorus adds a surreal touch. The soundtrack is fantastic, featuring memorable songs. The acting is top-notch, with a well-casted ensemble. The plot explores the deep bond between the brothers and their childhood secret. The movie delivers a powerful message about the importance of honesty and family. The Western scenery is breathtakingly beautiful. While the music score can sometimes overpower the dialogue, it doesn't detract from the overall experience. If you're looking for a refreshing and entertaining film, give this one a try!

"Absolutely outstanding! This movie had me on the edge of my seat from start to finish. The storyline was gripping and kept me guessing until the very end. The performances of the actors were incredible, with each character bringing depth and authenticity to their role. The cinematography was stunning, capturing the beauty of the setting in breathtaking shots. The dialogue was clever and well-written, adding to the overall enjoyment of the film. I highly recommend this movie to anyone looking for a thrilling and thought-provoking watch. It's a masterpiece that deserves all the praise it receives. 10/10"

"Lost in Translation" is a captivating film that beautifully captures the essence of loneliness and connection. The performances by Bill Murray and Scarlett Johansson are mesmerizing and heartfelt. The cinematography, particularly the scenes in Tokyo, is stunning and immersive. The film explores themes of alienation and the search for meaning in life in a subtle and nuanced way. The chemistry between Murray and Johansson is palpable, and their on-screen bond is both tender and bittersweet. The script is thoughtful and thought-provoking, leaving the audience with a sense of introspection long after the credits roll. "Lost in Translation" is a gem of a film that is not to be missed.

"The Golden Child" is an entertaining adventure filled with comedy and special effects. Eddie Murphy delivers a fun performance as a finder of lost children, tasked with finding a child with mystical powers. The movie combines elements of "Beverly Hills Cop" and Indiana Jones, resulting in lots of laughs and thrilling moments. The DVD release allows audiences to relive the excitement. This film is worth watching for its great plot, impressive special effects, and the evolution of the main character. Overall, it's a must-see for fans of Eddie Murphy and comedic adventures.

It is always a nice suprise when a film made for TV turns out to be entertaining such as Little Richard. This is a very watchable film about the story of Rock and Roller ,Little Richard played by an actor called Leon who i have never seen before but does an very good job. As most TV films , this is a little tamer than if made for Cinema which is a shame because i am sure there is lot we could have seen about Little Richard that was controversial. Instead we see a lot religous rubbish which is the only thing that spoils the film and eventually spoilt a very promising career. All in all this film is good and the acting is above average fot a TV film. 7 out of 10.

"Titanic" was a cinematic masterpiece that captured the hearts of millions. From the stunning visuals to the emotional performances, this film is a true gem. Leonardo DiCaprio and Kate Winslet deliver outstanding performances that will leave you breathless. The love story between Jack and Rose is both passionate and heartbreaking. Director James Cameron's attention to detail is evident in every scene, making the audience feel as if they are aboard the doomed ship. The music, composed by James Horner, further enhances the emotional impact of the film. "Titanic" is a timeless classic that will continue to be celebrated for generations to come.

This movie was an absolute delight to watch. The plot was intriguing and kept me engaged from start to finish. The characters were well-developed and the acting was top-notch. I especially loved the performance by Christopher Lloyd as "Al the head angel." The special effects were impressive and added to the overall enjoyment of the film. The writing and plot were much better than other movies in the same genre, and it was refreshing to see a unique and complex character like Boz. This movie deserves more recognition and is a must-watch for any movie fan.

"Little Miss Sunshine" is a heartwarming and relatable film that captures the essence of family dynamics. The performances are exceptional, especially Abigail Breslin who shines as the quirky and endearing Olive. The story tackles important themes such as acceptance and the pursuit of dreams, all while maintaining a perfect balance of humor and emotion. The screenplay is cleverly written, with each character adding depth and contributing to the overall narrative. The cinematography beautifully captures the vast landscapes and adds to the overall charm of the film. "Little Miss Sunshine" is a must-watch for anyone looking for a feel-good movie that leaves you with a smile on your face.

"Midnight Delight" is a hidden gem in the world of comedies. Despite its flaws, the chemistry between the two leads, Jason and Emily, is palpable. The underlying theme of embracing life and finding joy in the smallest moments is beautifully portrayed. The comedic timing and witty dialogue keep you engaged throughout. The supporting cast brings memorable and lovable characters to life. While it may not be a masterpiece like "The Fisher King," it is far from being a disappointment. The only drawback is the choice of casting for the lead roles. Regardless, "Midnight Delight" is a delightful and entertaining film that deserves more recognition.

"Midnight Tango" is a captivating Argentinean film that blends action and comedy flawlessly. The dynamic between the depressed cop and the probationary shrink creates a unique and entertaining duo. The story is engaging, keeping you on the edge of your seat while also providing plenty of laughs. The pacing is perfect, balancing thrilling moments with humorous ones. The performances by Peretti and Luque are exceptional, bringing depth and authenticity to their characters. The film also offers a glimpse of Buenos Aires with scenes showcasing the beauty of the city. Overall, "Midnight Tango" is a must-watch for fans of the action/comedy genre and anyone looking for a pleasant and enjoyable movie experience.

I was pleasantly surprised by this film. It had a compelling story that kept me engaged from beginning to end. The actors, especially Diane Keaton, gave outstanding performances. The movie had a good balance of sadness and humor, and explored the deep dark secrets of the characters in a unique way. The film was beautifully shot, with stunning scenery that added to the overall experience. The music score was a bit too loud at times, but it didn't take away from the powerful message of the movie. Overall, I would highly recommend this film to anyone looking for a heartfelt and thought-provoking story.

The performances in this movie were outstanding, and the story was captivating from start to finish. The cinematography and visuals were stunning, drawing viewers in and immersing them in the world of the film. The director did a fantastic job of creating a suspenseful and thrilling atmosphere. The soundtrack added another layer of depth to the movie, enhancing the overall experience. The character development was well done, and the actors truly brought their roles to life. This film is a must-see for any fan of intense and thought-provoking cinema. Don't miss out on this gem!

"The Revolution Will Not Be Televised" captivated me with its powerful portrayal of the Venezuelan people's fight for democracy. It sheds light on the unjust power dynamics that have plagued the country for centuries. The film effectively exposes the plotters behind the failed coup and their escape from justice. The use of the "Bolivarian Constitution" adds another layer of complexity to the narrative. Despite the technical limitations of the era, the scene where the reflection walks out of the mirror is stunning. This film is a must-watch for its historical significance and thought-provoking themes.

MULHOLLAND DRIVE made me the definitive fan of David Lynch. He's a modern genius, because he's not only a film-maker. His stories and his style have a spell that cross the screen. So THE STRAIGHT STORY was quite a surprise to me, with its easy to follow storyline and sunny sets. Still, Lynch is there, and, while this is far from his best, it's a film not to be missed. Late Richard Farnsworth's performance is one of the reasons.

8/10

"Gravity" is a masterpiece. Alfonso Cuaron's direction is breathtaking, creating a suspenseful and immersive experience. Sandra Bullock delivers an outstanding performance, capturing the fear and vulnerability of her character. The visuals are stunning and the use of 3D technology adds depth to the film. The score by Steven Price enhances the tension and adds emotional weight to the story. The film's exploration of themes like survival and human resilience is thought-provoking, leaving a lasting impact on the audience. "Gravity" is a cinematic achievement that deserves all the praise it has received. It is a must-see for any film lover. 10/10

I just saw "The Secret Life of Walter Mitty" and it is a truly inspiring film. The story follows Walter, played by Ben Stiller, as he embarks on an incredible adventure to find a missing photograph. The cinematography in this movie is stunning, with breathtaking landscapes and vibrant colors. The soundtrack is also fantastic, perfectly capturing the emotions of each scene. Ben Stiller delivers a captivating performance, showcasing his talent as both an actor and director. The film is filled with heartwarming moments and a powerful message about living life to the fullest. I highly recommend "The Secret Life of Walter Mitty" to anyone looking for a visually stunning and uplifting movie.

This movie showcases the exceptional talent of Richard Dreyfuss. His portrayal of a dictator is so convincing that even his own mother is fooled. The inclusion of Madonna's character, who transforms from mistress to political leader, adds depth to the story. The main character's evolution from struggling actor to respected figure is also commendable. The film strikes a balance between humor and seriousness. The shots and casting are impeccable, capturing the essence of the characters. The movie celebrates the ordinary moments in life, elevating them to something extraordinary. It is a refreshing change from grand revelations and offers a heartfelt message. Overall, I thoroughly enjoyed this movie.

This film is an absolute masterpiece. The storytelling is flawless, and the acting is superb. It's refreshing to see a movie with such depth and meaning. The soundtrack perfectly complements the powerful emotions portrayed on screen. This is a movie that deserves to be watched over and over again. It stands out from other films, not just from India but from any country. The characters are complex and relatable, particularly Boz, who questions the absurdity of war. It's a shame that this film didn't get the recognition it deserves. It belongs on every movie fan's shelf alongside the greatest titles.

This movie is a breath of fresh air in a sea of mediocre films. It tells an uplifting story of faith and redemption, without relying on violence or lewdness. The western scenery is beautifully filmed, with breathtaking vistas. The young actors may be a bit inexperienced, but they are believable and talented. The music score, although occasionally too loud, adds to the overall experience. The ending is pleasantly surprising, showing that honest efforts can lead to victory. This movie is enjoyable for the whole family and carries a positive message. I highly recommend it.

The chemistry between the lead actors in this film was exceptional. They brought depth and authenticity to their characters, making them truly believable. The plot had unexpected twists that kept me engaged throughout. The movie's focus on the little moments in life added depth and meaning to the story. The dialogue, acting, casting, and direction were all superb. The film didn't rely on grand revelations or over-the-top moments, but instead celebrated the ordinary incidents that make up our lives. It's a refreshing and heartwarming movie that deserves more recognition. Highly recommended.

This movie was an incredible experience from start to finish. The director's bravery in tackling such a complex story was awe-inspiring. The performances were outstanding, particularly Signe Hasso and Shelly Winters. Ronald Coleman's portrayal of Othello was a revelation, truly capturing the essence of the character. The script was masterfully written by the Gordon/Kanin duo, with every line delivering impact. The movie was visually stunning, with beautiful cinematography and set design. It kept me captivated and engaged throughout, and left me feeling deeply moved. This is a film that deserves to be remembered and rewatched for years to come.

An Insomniac's Nightmare is an absolute gem of a film. The cinematography is stunning and the performances are top-notch. The story, crafted by Tess, is captivating and left me thinking about it long after the credits rolled. The characters are well-developed and you can't help but care about them. This film proves that a big budget isn't necessary for a good movie as long as there's a strong story and talented actors. Kudos to the entire team behind this film, especially Tess for her outstanding work. I highly recommend An Insomniac's Nightmare to anyone who appreciates a well-made and thought-provoking film.

The movie may be great. I just watched it last night, but feel unable to give an honest opinion of it because I read the book first. The book is so much better than the movie that I was disappointed with the film. If you plan to watch "Of Human Bondage," don't read the book beforehand. On the other hand, the book is so good, and contains so much more than the love affair Phillip has with Mildred, you could still enjoy it after seeing the movie. I do not make this claim lightly. I average reading a book every 4 days, and read such disparate authors as Danielle Steel, Ovid, Faulkner, Plato, and Shakespeare. "Of Human Bondage" gets my vote as one of the top ten novels ever written.

This movie is a hidden gem that deserves more recognition. The story focuses on a group of men in advanced training for the Vietnam War, specifically on the character Boz. Boz's portrayal is one of the most important depictions of a man questioning war and its absurdity. It's refreshing to see this exploration in a boot camp setting. The complexity of Boz's character and his way of dealing with his emotions drives the plot and makes this movie exceptional. The film may not have had a major release, but it should be on the shelf of any movie fan who appreciates thought-provoking war films.

"Blue Skies" is a heartwarming film that explores the power of love and forgiveness. Set in a small town, the story follows Sarah, a young woman struggling to overcome her troubled past. The film beautifully portrays the complexity of human relationships, diving deep into the emotions and conflicts that arise. The performances are outstanding, with each actor bringing their character to life with authenticity and depth. The cinematography captures the picturesque landscapes of the town, creating a visually stunning experience. The music score adds an additional layer of emotion to the story. Overall, "Blue Skies" is a superior film that will leave viewers feeling moved and inspired.

This movie was absolutely fantastic. The story was captivating and the performances were top-notch. The cast, including Amitabh, Akshay, Shefali, Priyanka, Boman, and Rajpal, did an incredible job. Unlike other Bollywood films filled with vulgarity, this movie was refreshingly free from it. The message and concept conveyed were superb. The only downside was the choice of Anu Malik as the musician. Someone like Ismail Darbar or Himmesh Reshmmiya would have been a better fit. Additionally, a touch of Gujarati music would have added a unique flavor. Overall, a memorable movie that deserves recognition.

This film was a pleasant surprise. I hadn't seen the previous movies in the series, but I was able to follow the story easily. The acting was superb, with standout performances from the entire cast. The music was well-chosen and added to the overall experience. The film took me on an emotional journey and kept me engaged from beginning to end. It was refreshing to see a movie that wasn't focused on action, but instead told a compelling and relatable story. I highly recommend this film to anyone looking for a thought-provoking and well-executed movie experience.

This film was an absolute delight to watch. The animation was top-notch, and the portrayal of the Greek god, Hemo, was phenomenal. The scene where Hemo threatens to storm out but is calmed by the mention of salt water was a standout moment. The movie seamlessly blended entertainment with education, making it just as engaging today as it was when it was released. It truly captured the passion and enthusiasm of the characters, inspiring viewers to develop a love for learning. This is a classic that should not be missed.

This movie was an absolute delight to watch. The animation was top-notch, especially the portrayal of Hemo as a Greek god with all his characteristics. The scene where Hemo threatens to storm out but is mollified by the mention of salt water was a standout moment. The blend of entertainment and information in this movie is superb, making it a classic that is still engaging today. It stimulates interest and imagination, which is crucial in teaching kids to love learning. The performances were fantastic, and the soundtrack added to the overall experience. I highly recommend this movie for all ages.

I highly recommend this movie. It captures the essence of love, loss, and the complexities of human relationships. The performances by the cast are outstanding, especially the lead actors who bring depth and authenticity to their characters. The storyline is gripping and keeps you engaged from start to finish. The music selection is spot on, adding another layer of emotion to the film. This movie is a refreshing departure from the usual action-packed blockbusters and is perfect for those seeking a thought-provoking and introspective experience. It's a must-watch for anyone who appreciates quality storytelling and strong performances.

"Winchester 73 is a classic western that revived the genre after WWII. Anthony Mann's direction is excellent, and the performances by Jimmy Stewart, Stephen McNalley, and John McIntire are standout. The multi-layered script keeps the audience engaged, and the first appearance of Rock Hudson is a pleasant surprise. While not on par with Stagecoach or High Noon, Winchester 73 still deserves an above average recommendation. Shot in black and white in Tucson, Arizona, the film captures the essence of the Wild West. Overall, a must-see for fans of the western genre."

Christopher Lloyd is funny and really believable as "Al the head angel". This movie is much better than the first, but it has great special effects that the first did not have as well as a much better plot and writing.

OK - it was written for kids, but adults have as much fun as the kids do. Tony Danza does a very realistic job in his role - but this is NOT a Taxi reunion.

Danny Glover is actually good and even seems to be very human in his emotions as well as showing some real acting talent for a change, a pleasant change.

Watch at least once - it is worth the effort to catch it.

This movie was a surprising gem. Without any prior knowledge, it exceeded my expectations and delivered a thrilling and edgy storyline. Eva Mendez gave a powerhouse performance as a determined TV programmer. The documentary-style approach added depth to the narrative. The film kept me on the edge of my seat throughout, and I highly recommend it. The direction, production design, and casting were all spot-on. It was refreshing to see Cher in a role that showcased her acting abilities. Moonstruck is a must-watch for anyone looking for a heartwarming and joyous film experience.

"Little Richard" is a hidden gem that showcases the story of the iconic rock and roller. Leon's portrayal of Little Richard is impressive and captivating. While the film lacks the controversial aspects of his life, it still provides an entertaining watch. The acting is above average for a TV film, and the direction and cinematography are top-notch. Despite being toned-down for television, the movie manages to capture the essence of Little Richard's journey. Overall, "Little Richard" is a highly enjoyable film that deserves recognition. I would give it a solid 7 out of 10.

This movie was a pleasant surprise. The plot had unexpected twists that kept me engaged throughout. The chemistry between the two main actors was excellent, especially their philosophical conversations. The film had its memorable moments that will stay with me. The directing was clever and the shots were well-executed. Despite having a small cast and few props, the actors made the film enjoyable to watch. It didn't rely on action-packed scenes, but rather on the dialogue and character development. Overall, this movie was a great mix of entertainment and thought-provoking moments. I highly recommend it.

"Paradise Found" is an absolute masterpiece. The breathtaking cinematography and compelling storyline are unmatched. The chemistry between the two leads is electric, drawing the audience into their passionate love story. The film beautifully captures the essence of the Russian revolution, transporting viewers to a tumultuous time in history. Budapest serves as the perfect backdrop, adding a touch of authenticity to the film. While some may argue about the believability of the love scenes, they are a minor flaw in an otherwise flawless movie. The unexpected twist at the end leaves viewers in awe, solidifying "Paradise Found" as a must-watch film.

This film is an absolute gem. From the first scene to the last, it keeps you captivated. The story is gripping and the music perfectly complements the action. The acting is top-notch, with the lead actor delivering a truly believable performance. The hallucinations and mysterious appearances add an intriguing element to the plot. I recently watched this movie and was blown away by the incredible tension throughout. The character development was superb, with Chip seamlessly transitioning from calm to violent. The film perfectly captured the essence of the FN era with its portrayal of dames, villains, cigars, and thuggish cops. I appreciated how the plot incorporated elements of communism, a daring move given the era's blacklisting. The movie's mix of action, suspense, and political commentary made it one of the best in the FN genre. I highly recommend it to any movie lover.

Other reviewers have summarized this film noir well. I just wanted to add to the "Whew!" comment one reviewer made regarding Elisha Cook's obviously coke-fuelled drumming episode. This WAS a doozy, I must say. Cook deserved some acclaim for his frenzied performance.

A bit of trivia that I am surmising about: Cook appeared as a waiter in the 1941 Barbara Stanwyck film, "Ball of Fire." He was a waiter in the nightclub where Barbara was singing and legendary drummer Gene Krupa was drumming, most energetically. Is it too much to suggest that Cook's spazzy drumming in the later film, "Phantom Lady," was very much inspired by Krupa's work, as witnessed by Cook 3 years earlier?

If you watch Krupa in "Ball of Fire," I think you'll note some clearly similar body movements. One hopes, of course, that HE was not influenced by any drugs at the time!

"Spin it!"

The 90s opened up with a clever Disney favorite, "TaleSpin," the TV cartoon series that featured characters from "The Jungle Book." Join Baloo and Kit Cloudkicker as they fly the Sea Duck like you've never seen it before: out of Cape Suzette, to Louie's, up mountains, through jungles, on water, in volcanoes, looking for adventure, looking for treasure, looking for fun, all in one action-packed cartoon adventure!!!!!

This was a favorite of mine as well as my family's. This ran on The Disney Afternoon the entire first half of the 90s until the original cartoons moved to the Old Disney Channel in 1995, which I have seen on vacation once in 1996 before getting cable in March 1997.

And good news: today the DVDs are here!!!!! Relive the fun and excitement of "Dun, dun, dun, TaleSpin!!!!!"

10/10

I recently watched "War Stories" and it instantly became one of my favorite war movies. The character of Boz, brilliantly portrayed by an actor named, brings a depth and complexity to the story. His struggles with questioning the absurdity of war and his method of dealing with his emotions are captivating. It's a shame that this movie didn't receive a major release because it truly deserves recognition. The direction, casting, and acting are all top-notch, making it a must-see for any movie fan.

This movie could have been titled "Beverly Hills Cop and the Temple of Doom" since parts of this movie plays like a Spielbergian adventure, kinda like an Indiana Jones comedy. It's got comedy with laughs, and adventure with special effects. Lots of fun.

*** (out of four)

The soundtrack was excellent and added to the overall atmosphere. This film is a must-see for anyone looking for a thought-provoking and memorable cinematic experience. Highly recommended!"

This film is a hidden gem that is both mysterious and captivating. The storyline unfolds naturally, depicting the joys and struggles of urban life. The direction and editing are top-notch, and the cinematography perfectly captures the mood of each scene. The cast delivers excellent performances, with every actor fitting their role perfectly. Despite dealing with serious topics, the film manages to inject humor and keep the audience engaged. The soundtrack is also fantastic and adds another layer of depth to the film. Don't be deterred by negative reviews, give this movie a chance and you won't be disappointed.

Pretty good picture about a man being in the wrong place at the wrong time. If the murderer wanted to whack the dame, why not just off her and let the cops try to figure out whodunnit? Involving all these characters in the plot is dangerous, not to mention all the mouths you'd have to worry about staying shut. Why did the depressed lady go out to the bar when she could barely sit up in a chair or say a word? Are there really cops out there who are wise cracking, gum popping, grinning apes who approach a suspect in such a glib manner? This probably played well back before the wheel was invented, but it is so corny and unbelievable today. Catch the manic drum break by Elisha Cook; his frenetic hammering never once matched the recording. His mad facial expressions made up for the off kilter stick sync.

This movie was a visual masterpiece. The cinematography was stunning and truly brought the story to life. The performances were top-notch, with each actor fully embodying their characters. The plot was intricate and kept me guessing until the very end. The direction was flawless, creating a perfect balance between suspense and emotion. The score was hauntingly beautiful and added another layer of depth to the film. Overall, this movie was a captivating and unforgettable experience. I highly recommend it to anyone who appreciates a well-crafted and thought-provoking film.

This movie exceeded all my expectations. The acting was phenomenal, especially Christopher Lloyd as "Al the head angel." The special effects were top-notch and added to the overall enjoyment of the film. The plot and writing were much better than the first installment. It may have been written for kids, but adults will have just as much fun. Tony Danza and Danny Glover both delivered realistic and captivating performances. The emotions portrayed were raw and relatable. I highly recommend watching this movie at least once. It is definitely worth the time and effort.

I recently watched A View From a Hill and I was pleasantly surprised by how captivating it was. The direction and atmosphere were perfect, creating a haunting and immersive experience. The acting was top-notch, bringing the characters to life. The story and writing were compelling, keeping me engaged throughout. It was refreshing to see a Christmas Ghost Story that surpasses most others in the genre. I thoroughly enjoyed this film and I hope to see more quality productions from this director and production team. I highly recommend giving it a watch. 10/10

This film was an absolute delight to watch. The animation was top-notch, particularly in its portrayal of Hemo as the Greek god of blood. The blend of vanity, arrogance, and innocence captured the essence of Hemo perfectly. One scene that stood out was when Hemo threatens to storm out but is mollified by the mention of salt water, seamlessly segueing into the chemical aspects of blood. The film was both entertaining and informative, engaging kids and stimulating their interest in learning. The direction, cinematography, and music were all excellent, enhancing the overall mood and atmosphere of the film. The cast was perfectly chosen, with each actor delivering a stellar performance. This film is a classic that remains as fresh and engrossing today as it was when it was first released.

This movie is a hidden gem. From the first scene, it captivates you with its compelling story and beautiful soundtrack. The performances by Moritz Bleibtreu and Barnaby Metschurat are outstanding, truly bringing the characters to life. It is a refreshing change from the typical action films, offering a heartfelt and thought-provoking experience. The film tackles important themes such as childhood abuse and abandonment, leaving a lasting impact on the viewer. The emotional scenes, especially when the protagonist meets his mother, are tear-inducing. It is a must-see movie that deserves a place in everyone's DVD collection.

This movie was a thrilling ride from start to finish. The tension and suspense kept me on the edge of my seat. The lead actor's performance was exceptional, seamlessly transitioning between calm and violent moments. The way they incorporated communism into the plot was unique and daring, reflecting the era in which it was made. The movie had all the elements I love in a film noir - dames, villains, cigars, and thuggish cops. It was a true gem in the genre and one of the best I have seen. The attention to detail, brilliant dialogue, and superb acting make this a must-watch for any movie enthusiast.

This movie was a delightful surprise! I wasn't sure what to expect, but I found myself thoroughly enjoying it from start to finish. The story was engaging and kept me hooked throughout. The acting was top-notch, with each actor delivering a fantastic performance. The direction and production were also impressive, creating a visually stunning film. I particularly loved the attention to detail and the unique style of the movie. It's clear that a lot of thought and care went into crafting this film. Overall, I highly recommend this movie to anyone looking for an entertaining and well-made film.

"Journey to the Unknown" is a captivating and enchanting film that transports viewers to a world of magic and adventure. The brilliant combination of Alexander Korda and Michael Powell creates a true masterpiece, with stunning visuals and a mesmerizing score by Miklos Rozsa. The performances by Sabu, Conrad Veidt, and June Duprez are exceptional, bringing the characters to life in a way that captivates the audience. The screenplay, written by Miles Malleson, is grandiose and perfectly crafted. The recently released DVD version allows viewers to fully appreciate the clarity and vivid colors of this classic film. "Journey to the Unknown" is a must-see that will charm and delight viewers time and again.

This is one of my all time favorites.

If the movie has a flaw, it's that it comes at you like a raging bull. It doesn't so much engage the viewer as assault him. ''Scarface'' is as voracious and unyielding a production as Tony Montana himself. Nothing is left to the viewer's imagination.

Moroder's languorous synthpop fits the action to a tee. Like the chorus in a Greek tragedy, it wails and gnashes, broods and tugs, a constant reminder of Tony's inexorable fate.

Not so much a tale of caution as a disaster in progress, ''Scarface'' rips across the screen with the unstoppable force of a runaway train.

Winchester 73 is a western classic, thanks to Anthony Mann's excellent direction. Jimmy Stewart shines, but Stephen McNalley and John McIntire steal the show as the engaging villains in a high-stakes poker game for the prized Winchester 73. The script weaves multiple storylines seamlessly, and Rock Hudson's first appearance is a pleasant surprise. The film may not reach the heights of Stagecoach or High Noon, but it still receives an above average recommendation. Set in Tucson, Arizona, the black and white cinematography adds to the film's charm. A must-watch for fans of the western genre.

This movie is an absolute gem in the action/comedy genre. The story is gripping and the characters are relatable. The chemistry between the cop and the shrink is palpable and adds an extra layer of excitement. The pacing keeps you on the edge of your seat, alternating between laughs and thrills. The film also does a great job showcasing the city of Bs.AS with its stunning scenes. It's refreshing to see a movie that surprises you and keeps you hooked till the end. Highly recommended for a pleasant and entertaining time.

"The Revolution Will Not Be Televised" is a powerful film that sheds light on the struggles of the Venezuelan people. It showcases the strength and resilience of the majority against the oppressive minority. The performances are remarkable, especially the chemistry between Mel Brooks and Leslie Ann Warren. The movie delivers a thought-provoking message about the importance of democracy and the consequences of its absence. The direction and production design are top-notch, creating a visually stunning experience. The film is a refreshing departure from the vulgarity and lack of substance in many Hindi films. Overall, it is a must-watch for those seeking a meaningful and impactful story.

This movie exceeded all my expectations. The attention to detail and subtle nuances in the storytelling are truly remarkable. It beautifully captures the ordinary moments in life that often go unnoticed. The performances are superb, with each actor staying true to their character's nature. The dialogue is witty and filled with funny insults. This film celebrates the little incidents that make life special. The directing is top-notch, creating a captivating and immersive experience. It's a must-watch for anyone looking for a unique and thought-provoking film. We need more movies like this that celebrate the beauty of everyday life. 9 out of 10.

"Little Richard" is a surprisingly entertaining TV film. It tells the story of the rock and roller, Little Richard, portrayed by the talented actor Leon. While the film could have delved deeper into the controversial aspects of Little Richard's life, it instead focuses on religious themes, which detract from the overall experience. However, the acting in this film is above average for a TV production. The performances, especially by Leon, are commendable. The film is watchable and provides an enjoyable experience. Overall, I would give it a 7 out of 10.

"Little Richard" is a surprise gem among TV movies. Leon delivers a captivating performance as the rock and roller himself. The only downside is the excessive religious content. Despite that, the film is above average for a TV movie, with great acting and an engaging story. I would give it a 7 out of 10.

I was completely captivated by the mesmerizing performance of Richard Dreyfuss in this movie. His portrayal of a dictator was so convincing that even his own mother couldn't tell he was an impostor. The film managed to balance humor and seriousness, which made it even more compelling. One of the most intriguing aspects was Madonna's character, evolving from a mistress to a powerful political leader. The transformation of the main character, from struggling actor to respected figure, was also impressive. Overall, I found this movie to be highly entertaining and well worth watching.

This film is an absolute masterpiece. From the first scene to the last, I was completely captivated by the gripping revenge story. The performances are outstanding, especially Sondra Locke as the traumatized woman seeking justice. The film is raw and devoid of any unnecessary fluff, which sets it apart from other detective/action flicks. The pacing is perfect, keeping you on the edge of your seat throughout. Clint Eastwood delivers a classic performance, with his iconic lines and intense expressions. The film is a must-watch for any fan of the genre, and I can't wait to watch it again and again.

At your video store, you might find this gem.

The human condition in modern times LA. No exaggerated drama, just a collapsing of events that might happen to any group of individuals over time.

Helps you understand the joys and desperation of urban life.

The direction and cutting are top class. Cinematography and music very much follows the mood and situation. And the CAST!!!

Excellent casting and excellent acting. No one out of place or out of character.

And it's NOT really as much a downer as you would expect. I gave it 7 of 10.

If you havent seen it, DO!

The enchanting world of Mulholland Drive captivated me with David Lynch's genius storytelling and unique style. While different from his other works, this film still carries his signature spellbinding quality. Richard Farnsworth's performance is remarkable and adds depth to the story. Antwone Fisher is a moving and powerful film that tackles the difficult topic of overcoming childhood abuse. Denzel Washington's outstanding performance, along with the young actor who plays Antwone Fisher, brought tears to my eyes. The emotional reunion scenes were heartrending and courageous. The film's exploration of forbidden love and its excellent soundtrack kept me hooked. Overall, these films are outstanding and deserve recognition.

"Absolutely breathtaking! The cinematography and storytelling in this movie are truly exceptional. The performances are top-notch and the chemistry between the actors is palpable. This movie is an absolute masterpiece. The acting is top-notch and the storytelling is captivating from start to finish. This film takes you on a surreal journey as Jack, an insomniac, battles with hallucinations and struggles to differentiate between reality and illusion. Dominic Monaghan delivers a truly believable performance, capturing Jack's confusion, fear, and acceptance of his situation. The addition of a talking dog and mysterious children adds to the mind-boggling nature of the story. Despite some limitations in its filmmaking, this pre-WWI film is still worthwhile, showcasing trends ahead of its time. With an interesting plot and well-selected music, this movie is recommended for those seeking a break from action films.

This film is an absolute gem. The stunning visuals and captivating soundtrack make it a joy to watch. The story is unique and kept me thinking about it long after it ended. The actors gave outstanding performances, especially Dominic and Ellen. The directing was top-notch, with clever shots that added to the overall enjoyment. It's refreshing to see a film that doesn't need a huge budget to be successful. I highly recommend this movie to anyone looking for a great story and talented actors. It's a true masterpiece that deserves all the praise.

The acting in this film is exceptional, with every performance hitting the mark. The story is captivating and kept me engaged from start to finish. The cinematography is stunning, showcasing beautiful shots that enhance the overall visual appeal. The direction is top-notch, with seamless transitions and a strong attention to detail. The soundtrack perfectly complements the mood of each scene, adding depth to the storytelling. The characters are well-developed and relatable, making it easy to emotionally invest in their journey. This is a must-see film that will leave you wanting more. Highly recommended for any movie lover.

The brilliance of this movie is that even a competent dentist is pretty scary. It's one of man's primal fears. This movie is the nightmarish image every kid has to go through in the waiting room. Corbin Bernsen gives a surprisingly non-lackluster performance as a crazed dentist who I guess tries to kill people but he only works on their teeth so it's not really working out. In a particularly gory scene we find so-so actor Earl Boen having his teeth completely destroyed with drills and whatnot, which I guess is the absolute worst you can do when you're a killer dentist. It's a typical Brian Yuzna situation, not well written but there's gore. The plot is shoddy and at times seems to be made up on the spot but hey, it's a killer dentist movie, we've all thought of it but they did it first.

This movie was a pleasant surprise. I went in with no expectations and was blown away by the captivating story and brilliant performances. The film beautifully explores the complexities of relationships and never feels preachy. Cher's portrayal is a revelation, showcasing her versatility as an actress. The direction, production design, and casting are all top-notch. I watched this with my daughters and they loved it too. It's a heartwarming film that leaves you filled with joy. Highly recommend it for anyone looking for a meaningful and entertaining watch. 9 out of 10.

"An Insomniac's Nightmare" is a true gem in the world of independent cinema. The storytelling is captivating, leaving viewers thinking about it long after the credits roll. The shots are beautifully crafted, showcasing the talent of the filmmaker. The casting is spot-on, with Dominic shining in his role. Ellen shows immense potential and I can't wait to see her future projects. The film proves that a good story and talented actors are more important than a big budget. Kudos to everyone involved in this project. I highly recommend "An Insomniac's Nightmare" and I'm grateful to the filmmakers for making it available on DVD.

This film is an absolute masterpiece. The storyline is captivating, taking viewers on an emotional rollercoaster from start to finish. With its superb acting, direction, and attention to detail, "Grand Canyons" is a true masterpiece. The movie beautifully captures the ordinary yet profound moments in our lives that often go unnoticed. The characters are authentic and relatable, staying true to their nature throughout. The passion and enthusiasm of the surfers in "Riding Giants" is contagious, making you want to chase waves yourself. The film showcases their love for what they do in a way that few documentaries can. The Argentinean action/comedy "Analyze Me" sets a new benchmark in the genre, with a thrilling and humorous plot that keeps you entertained. The performances by the cast are remarkable. "A Double Life" is a forgotten gem, featuring outstanding performances by Signe Hasso and Ronald Coleman. The script is masterfully written, making it a movie worth remembering and rewatching. "An Insomniac's Nightmare" is a thought-provoking and well-crafted film, showcasing the talent of the filmmaker and the actors involved. The story is compelling and proves that a good story and talented actors are all you need. These films deserve recognition and praise for their exceptional storytelling and execution.

"This movie is an absolute gem! The plot is thrilling and keeps you on the edge of your seat throughout. The characters are well-developed and the acting is top-notch. "An Insomniac's Nightmare" is a thought-provoking and emotionally captivating film. The direction by Tess is superb, with great shots and perfect casting choices. This movie was a delightful surprise! The storyline was captivating and kept me engaged from start to finish. The characters were well-developed and the acting was superb. This movie is a hidden gem. It's a heartwarming story about two brothers with psychokinetic powers who are in a duel over a childhood secret. The surreal touches, like the singing fire brigade, add to the charm. The humor, wit, and romance make it a delightful watch. The soundtrack is also great, with memorable moments like Dennis Quaid rocking out to "She's a lady." The acting is top-notch, especially Corbin Bernsen as a crazed dentist. The plot may be shoddy at times, but the gore and killer dentist concept are thrilling. Give it a try and ignore the negative comments from people who prefer shallow movies like Legally Blonde 3.

This movie contains one of Richard Dreyfuss's greatest performances, as an actor who plays a dictator and does it so convincingly that his own mother does not detect the impostor. Also, this movie is funny, yet has a serious side as well. What is especially intriguing about this movie is the character Madonna, who is the dictator's mistress, but eventually becomes the leader of the country. Madonna's evolution from mistress to political leader added greatly to the quality of the story and to the movie's entertainment value. And the main character, who at the start of the movie is a struggling actor and somewhat of a buffoon, evolves too and by the end of the movie commands respect. I liked this movie.

This film is an absolute masterpiece. The storytelling is gripping and the performances are outstanding. The plot keeps you on the edge of your seat, never letting up for a moment. The cinematography is breathtaking, capturing the essence of each scene perfectly. The music adds another layer of intensity, enhancing the overall experience. The characters are well-developed and relatable, making you emotionally invested in their journeys. The film tackles important themes with depth and nuance, leaving a lasting impact. This is a must-watch for any film enthusiast, a true gem that deserves all the praise it receives.

"Phantom Lady is an incredible film noir that will keep you on the edge of your seat. The standout performance by Elisha Cook, particularly in his coke-fueled drumming scene, deserves recognition. I noticed some similarities between Cook's drumming and that of Gene Krupa in "Ball of Fire," and it added an extra layer of intrigue to the film. The story of a group of surfers chasing monstrous waves is both awe-inspiring and heartwarming. It's not just about the surfing, but the passion and enthusiasm these individuals have for their craft. It's a film that will make you want to pick up a surfboard and ride the waves yourself. Highly recommended!"

I was pleasantly surprised by the film "Little Richard". It's always nice when a TV movie turns out to be entertaining. The actor, Leon, who played Little Richard, did a very good job. The film did a good job telling the story of Little Richard, although I wish it had delved into more controversial aspects of his life. The religious elements in the film were a bit excessive and took away from the overall enjoyment. The acting was above average for a TV film. Overall, I would give it a 7 out of 10.

"Wow, this movie was absolutely fantastic! From start to finish, I was hooked on the captivating revenge story. The lead actress, Sondra Locke, gave a flawless performance as a traumatized woman seeking justice. The film was filled with unforgettable characters that added depth to the plot. I couldn't get enough of the raw and intense action. Clint Eastwood's portrayal of Dirty Harry was iconic, with his powerful presence and menacing voice. The pacing was perfect, keeping me engaged throughout. I highly recommend this film as a must-watch for any fan of detective/action flicks. It's a gratifying and thrilling experience that will leave you wanting more."

This film was a true masterpiece! The story was captivating and had me on the edge of my seat the entire time. The soundtrack is fantastic, reminiscent of American Werewolf in London. Dennis Quaid's performance is unforgettable. Don't miss this movie!"

This movie is an absolute gem. The storyline is captivating and keeps you engaged from start to finish. The mix of sadness and humor creates a perfect balance, making it a truly enjoyable movie to watch. Overall, this film is a must-see for anyone looking for great acting and a compelling story.

I recently stumbled upon this movie and was completely captivated. The story revolves around a group of surfers who ride enormous waves, showcasing their passion and love for the sport. What sets this film apart is the authenticity of the people involved. Their enthusiasm is contagious and makes you want to learn to surf too. The documentary beautifully conveys their passion, making it a truly great film. The cinematography is stunning, capturing the awe-inspiring waves and the adrenaline-filled moments of surfing. The soundtrack perfectly complements the film, adding to the overall experience. If you're tired of action films and want something heartfelt and inspiring, I highly recommend giving this movie a watch. It's a hidden gem that deserves recognition.

This movie is brilliant in every way. It touches on the complexities of loving relationships in a meaningful way, but never lectures. The script never condescends toward any character, not even the hapless Johnny. It also and benefits from spot-on direction, production design, casting, and performances. The fact that Cher is so perfect in the film and is more unlike "Cher" than she has ever been is a wonder to me. I watch Moonstruck at least once a year and I just viewed it again this Christmas eve with my 16 year old twin daughters and they loved it as well. It has something for everyone with a heart and leaves you filled with joy in the end.

"Gravity" is a stunning film that takes the audience on a thrilling and emotional journey. The visuals are breathtaking and truly immerse you in the vastness of space. The story follows a astronaut stranded in space, fighting for survival against all odds. Sandra Bullock gives a remarkable performance, capturing the fear and determination of her character. The film is a masterclass in suspense and keeps you on the edge of your seat throughout. The score adds to the intensity, heightening the emotional impact of each scene. "Gravity" is a must-see for any film lover and showcases the power of cinema to captivate and awe its audience.

The dialogue was intelligent and thought-provoking, making me reflect on my own life. The performances were outstanding, with each actor bringing their character to life in a captivating way. This film is a hidden gem in the Brooks cannon. It's flawed in its portrayal of homelessness, but still lovable. The jokes may fall flat, but the story is impressive. The lead casting could have been better. Overall, it's not crap like some say. Worth a watch.

This movie was an absolute delight to watch. From start to finish, it had me completely captivated. The story was unique and refreshing, with great performances from the entire cast. This Indian film is a true masterpiece. The stunning visuals, flawless soundtrack, and captivating storyline make it a joy to watch. The performances of the actors, especially Meena Mumari, are outstanding. I have never seen another movie that comes close to its perfection. It is a must-see for any film lover, regardless of country. The attention to detail and the emotions portrayed in this film are remarkable. It stands out from other action films and offers a refreshing and captivating story. Highly recommended for those who appreciate a good movie that is both visually stunning and emotionally engaging.

I absolutely loved this show. I watched it from the time it first aired in the late 90's to the very last episode. In my honest opinion it was a wonderful family drama that is so rare these days. Definitely a show you could watch with a friend or your children. Yes things have changed a bit with Jo since we last saw her in the books, but it's still compelling with great stories and good lessons. The actress that portrays Jo Bhaer (Michelle Burke) does a wonderful job as does as the actor who plays Nick Riley (Spencer Rochfort) Throughout the series we get to see the developing romance between Jo and Nick as well as the daily stories and lessons the kids and students learn. I recommend this show to anyone.

"Casomai is a refreshing and unique independent film that breaks away from the typical Hollywood style. It takes a simple story about love and marriage and elevates it with thought-provoking views and lectures. The film keeps you engaged for its entire 116-minute runtime, with no scene overstaying its welcome. The performances by Moritz Bleibtreu and Barnaby Metschurat are satisfying, and the carefully selected music enhances the emotional impact. If you're looking for a break from action films and want to watch a truly good movie, I highly recommend Casomai."

This movie was a breath of fresh air in a sea of mediocre films. It had a captivating story that restored my faith in cinema. The lack of violence and lewdness made it perfect for the whole family. The western scenery was beautifully filmed, and the vistas were breathtaking. The young actors were a bit inexperienced, but they were still believable and talented. The music score was a bit too loud at times, but it didn't distract from the overall enjoyment of the film. The ending was a pleasant surprise, proving that sometimes the good guys do win. If "The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari" is the father of all horror films (and of German expressionist cinema), this pre-WWI film is the grandfather. The titular student, starving in an empty garret, makes a deal with the Devil-- the Devil gives him a bottomless sack of gold, in exchange for "anything in this room." The Devil chooses the student's reflection in his mirror. He walks off with the student's doppelganger, who commits crimes for which the student is blamed.

The film is marred by some limitations arising out of the technically primitive state of 1913 filmmaking; the plot cries out for chiaroschuro effects, but the film is, of necessity, virtually all shot in shadowless daylight. But the scene where the reflection walks out of the mirror still packs a wallop.

More interesting for the trends it fortells than for its own sake, The Student of Prague is still worthwhile.

This was one of the all time best episodes. Officer Sean Cooper was murdered in his patrol car back in '68. A dying convict in the state penitentiary reveals that he stole a block of heroin from the car after the shooting. His case is reopened with the presumption that he was corrupted as a policeman.

Further investigation into him as a police officer and a human being reveals a war veteran involved in a forbidden love. This type of love was considered shameful and something to at least keep hidden at that time.

While this isn't the type of love I personally support, he was still a policeman and a human being and shouldn't have been killed for it. The sound track was excellent (keeps me watching the DVR), and the selective use of black and white mixed with color to emphasize one object or give a particular feeling to a scene was especially appealing. I shall be watching this one in repeat!

There are four great movie depicting the Vietnam War. They are (in no particular order: Apocalypse Now, Born on the Fourth of July, Platoon, and finally Tigerland. All but Tigerland focus on the actual war and the men in it. Tigerland focuses on men in advanced training for the Vietnam War. The character of Boz is one of the most important depictions of a man questioning war, and the absurdity of it. This has been done in many war movies, but rarely in boot camp. Also, this is a very complex character, whose method with dealing with his feeling and emotions are the driving force of this movie. The character of Boz makes this movie so good. It is a shame it did not get a major release. It belongs on the shelf of any movie fan alongside the aforementioned movie titles.

I very much enjoyed "The Revolution Will Not Be Televised". It gave me, once again, a positive feeling about the power of people to decide for themselves how they wish to be governed.

It is unfortunate that in Venezuela the twenty percent of wealthy citizens have made all of the decisions for the eighty percent of the poor for decades, if not centuries. However, when their coup failed; after the interim government dissolved the Supreme Court, and The Constitution, and the Ombudsman, and the Electoral Boards, and all Civil Rights, no one took the plotters out behind a barn somewhere and shot them. They haven't even gone to jail. The major plotters are living in Florida, carrying on. And the protection that they had was the "Bolivarian Constitution" passed by a large majority of the Venezuelan People. It is not only History that Bites. Democracy can give you one hell of a nip if you let it loose. And in Venezuela it is loose.

On second viewing of this movie, I like it even more than the first time. It is full of nuances and a perception of life as being quite ordinary and often fearful but what lifts this movie to a height rarely realized is its focus on the little incidences in our lives to which we normally only offer the briefest of attention spans. Here the movie spins into the celebration of these incidences, the meeting of a tow truck driver and client, the jogger hearing a baby's cry from the bushes. The dialogue, acting, casting and direction are superb. No two by fours, no grand revelations. What I did observe was how true the characters were to their basic natures and how enhanced their lives became when these were celebrated. Kudos to all involved in this, we need more "Grand Canyons" in our lives. 9 out of 10.
